title: Make legacy instrument's toString spoofing undetectable to recursive toString.toString() checks
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Current state:

Desired state:

Reason:

Source: #1154 review item R31 (datadir/review-1154.md:267-273).

## Problem (page-observable detection vector)
To hide its wrappers, the LEGACY js-instrument mocks a wrapped function's `toString` so it
returns `[native code]` (the toString artifact at `Extension/src/lib/js-instruments.ts:442-453`).
A naive spoof is self-inconsistent and therefore detectable: the OVERRIDE function used to
produce `[native code]` exposes its OWN source under recursive inspection. A page can detect the
instrument by probing one level deeper:

- `obj.toString.toString()` must ALSO return `[native code]` (a naive mock returns the override
  function's own source instead).
- It must resist `Function.prototype.toString.call(obj.toString)` (re-applying the real
  `toString` to the mock must still yield `[native code]`).
- It must resist toString-identity checks (comparing/inspecting the mock against the genuine
  native `toString`).

Stealth gets this for free: `exportFunction`'s native passthrough means the exported function is
genuinely native all the way down, so recursive `toString` checks see `[native code]` at every
level. Legacy has no such passthrough and needs a self-consistent equivalent — native all the way
down — so that `obj.toString`, `obj.toString.toString`, and any `Function.prototype.toString.call`
re-application are all indistinguishable from a real native function.

## Goal
Make the legacy toString spoof undetectable to recursive `toString.toString()` and
`Function.prototype.toString.call(...)` checks: the spoofing mechanism must itself report
`[native code]` and survive toString-identity probes, with no level exposing the override
function's own source.

## Relationships
- Relates to the legacy toString artifact at `Extension/src/lib/js-instruments.ts:442-453`.
- Part of the legacy-footprint / de-pollution umbrella (ADR-0001 follow-up #3); sibling to the
  legacy error-stack sanitization (#53) and the call-attribution frame-filtering port (#54),
  which touch the same rethrow/footprint region.
- Strengthens the relax-mutual-exclusion path tracked in GH #1187.
